inventories.ticket: R0 — Backup safety (Wave A, first): verify prod S3 backup config, raise retention across the reconcile window, add restore-drill script/runbook, institutionalize rename-to-archive + delayed-drop for destructive migrations
inventories.summary: At HEAD, backup is a single in-process scheduler (server/backup.ts, started from server/startup.ts:33): first run 30s after boot, then every 24h (interval anchored to process start, so every deploy resets the clock). Each cycle writes DATA_DIR/backups/backup-<ts>/ (uploads copy excluding optimized/, dashboard.db via VACUUM INTO, _manifest.json with integrity_check + per-table row counts), then — only if BACKUP_S3_BUCKET is set — tars and uploads to s3://$BACKUP_S3_BUCKET/${BACKUP_S3_PREFIX:-backups}/backup-<ts>.tar.gz (region default us-east-1) and prunes S3 objects older than the SAME retention constant. Retention default is 3 days (BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS, backup.ts:25) driving BOTH local and S3 prunes; .env.example wrongly documents 7, storage-stats.ts displays default 7, and render.yaml declares BACKUP_S3_BUCKET/REGION + AWS keys (all sync:false, values only in Render dashboard) but does NOT declare BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS or BACKUP_S3_PREFIX — so prod runs the 3-day code default on a 1GB disk. No restore script exists; restore is a manual doc drill (docs/workflows/data-integrity-recovery.md §2) plus GET /api/admin/db-export, POST /api/admin/db-import (ALLOW_DB_IMPORT=true, staging only), and scripts/pull-render-latest-backup.sh. The migration runner (server/db/index.ts) is forward-only, single IMMEDIATE transaction with FK OFF, lexicographic order, MIGRATION_RENAMES alias bridge, tolerant per-statement ALTER handling — rollback is restore-based by design, which is exactly why R0 gates the reconcile waves.

inventories.dependencies: Verified beyond the ratified skeleton: (1) Retention default is contradictory at HEAD — backup.ts:25 default '3' (drives BOTH local and S3 prune from one constant), storage-stats.ts:206 displays default '7', .env.example:69 documents '7', health.ts:391 manual-prune default 3 — R0 must reconcile all four, not just raise a number. (2) render.yaml's shared-s3-backups group omits BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS and BACKUP_S3_PREFIX entirely, so prod runs code defaults unless someone set them in the Render dashboard; BUCKET/REGION/AWS keys are all sync:false, meaning 'verify prod S3 backup config' is irreducibly an ops action (Render dashboard + S3 console listing), not a repo check — recommend adding a lastBackupAt surface so it becomes monitorable. (3) The S3 prune deletes objects using the SAME constant as local prune and runs on every successful upload — any retention split must ensure the off-site prune uses the raised value or the raise is silently undone. (4) The skeleton missed that a restore-drill runbook already exists (docs/workflows/data-integrity-recovery.md §2-3, plus scripts/platform-data-integrity-recovery.ts with verify:data-integrity and the ARTIFACT_RECOVERY_MAP) — R0 should extend and script it, not create a parallel doc. (5) pr-check already has migration-SQL scanning + baseline-file infrastructure (buildWorkspaceScopedTables, findJsonArrayColumnSites, allowedMigrationFiles) and tests/pr-check.test.ts has synthetic-migrations-dir fixtures — the new rule is a pattern-fit, low-cost customCheck. (6) @aws-sdk/client-s3 is already a dependency (package.json:80), dynamically imported only by backup.ts — no npm install needed. (7) backup.ts reads BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS at module load, so env changes need a restart (Render restarts on env change — fine, but drills should note it). (8) Render disks are 1GB (both services) — local retention raise has a hard disk budget; each backup dir holds a full DB + uploads copy.
inventories.risks: Highest: prod may have NO working off-site backup today — BACKUP_S3_BUCKET is sync:false in render.yaml and unverifiable from the repo; if it was never set in the dashboard, production holds only 3 days of local backups on the same 1GB disk as the live DB, and every reconcile drop would be unrecoverable beyond 72h. The ops verification must happen before any other Wave A work. Second: the single RETENTION_DAYS constant drives the S3 DeleteObjects prune on every upload — a careless retention split that leaves the S3 prune reading the low local value would actively delete the raised-retention off-site copies (silent, logged only at info). Third: 1GB disk budget — raising local retention multiplies (full DB + uploads) per day; prefer S3-side raise + modest local, or bump sizeGB in the same PR. Fourth: the new pr-check rule will fail pr-check:all unless the 6 historical DROP TABLE migrations are baselined (allowedMigrationFiles-style). Fifth: backup interval is process-anchored, not clock-anchored — deploy-heavy reconcile weeks will produce extra backups (disk pressure) and the 'daily' cadence is really 'per 24h-of-uptime'; the drill/runbook should not assume a fixed daily timestamp. Sixth: tests/unit/backup.test.ts mocks fs and S3 entirely — green units prove nothing about restorability; only the staged drill (PR2) does. Minor: /api/admin/db-export reads the whole file via stream (fine) but wal_checkpoint(FULL) failure is swallowed — drill should verify the pulled DB's integrity, which the existing verify:data-integrity --db path already covers.

inventories.dependencies: "Verified at HEAD: (1) R3 is Barrier 2 — R4/R5/R6/R7 sit behind it, so the envelope's shape (especially any formalization of the rec lifecycle sub-axis) must not pre-empt the R4 owner decision; the two-axis contract (docs/rules/strategy-recommendations.md, pr-check rules 21/22, single-writer server/recommendation-lifecycle.ts) is a hard boundary R3 must leave intact. (2) The ratified skeleton MISSED: two parallel validators already in-tree (copy-review.ts isValidTransition, voice-calibration.ts LEGAL_STATUS_TRANSITIONS + its own VoiceProfileStateTransitionError class) — 'never build parallel' requires folding them, not just mapping new enums; the documented rank-tracking-reconciliation.ts guard bypass with live data (1 replaced row); THREE structural escape classes in the pr-check rule (literal '= x', mid-clause ', status =', non-'status' column names like resolution_status) — the audit's rule citation implies coverage the regex doesn't deliver; docs/rules/development-patterns.md documents a wrong validateTransition signature/return contract (2-arg, returns null — actual is 4-arg, throws); PageEditStatus is a priority lattice not a transition map, is duplicated in src/components/ui/statusConfig.ts, and local dev data contains out-of-union values; LocalSeoMarketStatus has NO status-update path at HEAD (insert-only classification, despite looking lifecycle-shaped). (3) Guard-coverage contract test (tests/contract/state-machine-guard-coverage-contract.test.ts) pins entity→file→token triples — the envelope must keep both 'validateTransition(' and the *_TRANSITIONS token greppable per file or update the test in the same commit. (4) 15 route/MCP files already translate InvalidTransitionError; every newly guarded store needs its callers checked for the same mapping. (5) MCP enum-rename exposure flagged by the audit belongs to R4/R5, not R3 — R3 adds no renames."
inventories.risks: "(1) Behavior change from new guards: writes that previously silently accepted illegal moves (re-dismiss a dismissed seo_suggestion, re-resolve a resolved insight, bulk MCP resolves, cron/orchestrator retries) will start throwing InvalidTransitionError — every new map needs deliberate idempotent self-edges or route-level no-op handling, and FM-2-style tests; the GBP awaiting_client→awaiting_client resend edge is the in-repo precedent. (2) page_edit_states legacy out-of-union values ('pending', 'in_river'—sic 'in_review') would make a strict guard throw on real rows; needs normalization or exemption before mapping (dev-DB evidence only; prod may differ — re-verify against staging via npm run db:sync-staging). (3) Extending the pr-check regex risks false positives (INSERT...ON CONFLICT SET, non-lifecycle status columns like OAuth health) — hatch ergonomics matter because above-line hatches are silently ignored (only inline works); follow pr-check-rule-authoring.md and land AFTER guards (PR ordering) or CI breaks. (4) Envelope refactor touching validateTransition's signature would fan out to 36 call sites + the guard-coverage test + ai-reliability-registry token — keep the 4-arg throwing API frozen and layer the envelope alongside. (5) Formalizing the rec lifecycle sub-axis table could be read as pre-deciding R4 — scope it as 'faithful model of current single-writer edges' only, with explicit doc language, or defer it to the R4 decision. (6) Census misclassification is the quiet failure mode: mapping a derived projection (KeywordCommandCenterStatus, ClientRequestStatus, approval batch aggregate) would CREATE the parallel-machine problem R3 exists to prevent — the census doc must record a verdict + evidence per union, and the envelope contract test should enforce lifecycle-only registration."

(3) The skeleton missed the second divergence direction: the act-on route never syncs the mirror, and respondToDeliverable for rec-type deliverables deliberately has no respondToSource (adapter comment declares rec.clientStatus source of truth — text that contradicts the ratified authority split and must flip). (4) The divergence sweep has a ready-made template: server/recommendation-staleness.ts + its outcome-crons.ts registration (dark flag 'strategy-staleness-scan'). (5) Lane collisions per audit §6: R10 shares server/recommendation-lifecycle.ts and shared/recommendation-predicates.ts — keep R4 lanes file-disjoint. (6) writeItems delete-then-reinsert means the trigger fires on every regen row — pre-existing bad data bricks regens, hence the PR3 verify step. inventories.risks: Highest: enabling the DB trigger before the app-layer guards (PR1) or before staging/prod data verification (PR3) turns a latent struck+completed row into a hard failure of EVERY regen (writeItems rewrites all rows in one transaction) — sequence strictly PR1 → PR3 → PR4. Second: making the mirror non-best-effort must NOT let a mirror failure roll back or 500 a live send — verifiability has to be additive (durable record + sweep), preserving the never-throws contract both modules document. Third: the act-on mirror-sync adds a write inside/around an existing carefully-ordered transaction (approve + content request atomically; side effects post-commit) — the mirror update must stay post-commit and idempotent. Fourth: dev-DB blind spot — 0 sent recs and 0 rec mirrors locally means the divergence sweep's real-data behavior is only observable on staging; the flag-ON staging soak in PR3 is load-bearing, not optional. Fifth: coverage-ratchet + ~135 contract tests pin adjacent shapes; the MirrorOutcome signature change ripples into at least 4 integration tests (budgeted above). Sixth: R10/R4 file collision if run in parallel lanes.

inventories.summary: R5 at HEAD (readiness-atlas-audit, 3b1358af0 = audit report on top of e5745db2b) is a code-only ticket: build one read-only action CATALOG (metadata registry keyed by context+action, modeled on BACKGROUND_JOB_METADATA's mapped-type shape in shared/types/background-jobs.ts:72-79) spanning five verified vocabularies — ActionType (17 members), RecType (15), ClientActionSourceType (5), KeywordCommandCenterActionType (7 verbs + 3 bulk + 4 next-action extensions), and the MCP action enums which actually live in shared/types/mcp-action-schemas.ts (not server/mcp/tools/*) — while never merging the typed unions and making the three existing seam mappers (recommendationOutcomeActionType, OUTCOME_ACTION_TYPE_BY_SOURCE, signalToRecType) plus two implicit seams (KCC add_to_strategy→strategy_keyword_added, insight-resolve→insight_acted_on) explicit catalog metadata. The sweep's 'two phantom ActionType entries' claim is refuted by grep+DB evidence: every one of the 17 members has a live producer path; the keep-markers are produced by ContentGaps.tsx:149/TopicClusters.tsx:103 and outcome-scoring-defaults.ts:79-83 says the opposite of the quoted 'vestigial' — so cleanup re-scopes to provenance documentation, and the concrete consolidation payload is instead the four duplicated Record<ActionType,string> label maps + CLIENT_ACTION_BADGES, plus a genuine out-of-union 'content_post' seed bug. Zero migrations; two PRs (additive catalog+contract-test, then behavior-preserving label consumption), staging-first, sequenced after R3.

inventories.risks: 1) Union-merge temptation: ScoringConfig = Record<ActionType,…> means any folded-in verb forces nonsense scoring entries — catalog must import the unions, never redefine them; mapped-type keys give compile-time completeness. 2) MCP external contract: 61 tools + long-lived per-workspace API keys (migration 163) — catalog must be strictly additive; renaming any wire enum (send/throttle/strike, respond statuses, KCC verbs) is a breaking change requiring the tolerate-old/emit-new workstream, out of R5 scope. 3) Phantom cleanup is refuted at HEAD: all 17 ActionType members have live producers; dropping topic_cluster_keep/content_gap_keep would break strategy-managed-set-keep.test.ts, the exhaustive ScoringConfig Record, and the live Keep buttons — the deliverable becomes 'document producer provenance in the catalog', not deletion. 4) Label consolidation must preserve exact strings and import paths (ACTION_TYPE_LABELS values are asserted verbatim in two integration tests; admin vs client wording deliberately differs per type → separate adminLabel/clientLabel fields; ui-vocabulary.md governs wording; client-facing labels also interact with R12's translation map — coordinate). 5) File collisions: server/routes/outcomes.ts is in R8's attribution scope and the R9-replacement coverage ticket — lanes must be file-disjoint per audit §6. 6) shared/types/index.ts is a type-only barrel; exporting a runtime const changes bundling for both client and server — verify tree-shaking/build after the barrel edit (typecheck + vite build gate). 7) Historical vocabulary: resolveWinTitle tolerates legacy aliases ('content_post'/'post', 'content_brief'/'brief') and tracked_actions rows carry write-time vocabulary — catalog should tag these as historical (R1 word classes), never retro-rename data. 8) InsightsEngine.tsx / recCategoryMap / recTypeTab / REC_POLICY_REGISTRY are compile-enforced Record<RecType,…> registries — leave them in place and reference from the catalog; consolidating them now would balloon the diff and risk client-facing churn.

inventories.dependencies: Verified at HEAD: (1) Direct blob SQL is confined to storage.ts + migrate-json.ts + tests — every runtime consumer (routes/recommendations.ts endpoints, MCP recommendation-actions.ts, 6 intelligence slices, recommendation-public-projection/client-insight-view-model, outcome-backfill/remediation/emv-calibration, work-orders + 5 webflow resolve paths, briefing/strategy-issue/meeting-brief/pov/admin-chat/the-issue-export/staleness) reads through the loadRecommendations facade or leaf loaders, so dual-read holds with zero consumer changes. (2) Things the ratified skeleton missed: (a) server/db/migrate-json.ts:818-827 is a SECOND blob writer — legacy JSON→SQLite environments would silently lose all recs post-cutover unless it emits rows (its test at tests/unit/db/migrate-json.test.ts:1249-1286 pins the current shape); (b) boot ordering — runOutcomeRemediation() in server/index.ts calls loadRecommendationSet at startup, so the new sweep must be wired before it; (c) the blob is ALREADY deliberately stale after any row-level mutation (replaceRecommendationItems/updateRecommendationItem never rewrite it — pinned by outcome-backfill-pure.test.ts:506-528), which makes the materializer's count>0 guard the critical correctness invariant for the backfill: running the copy against a workspace WITH rows would resurrect stale statuses/lifecycle; (d) recommendationSchema enum↔RecType lockstep must be confirmed before the backfill runs, because parseJsonSafeArray silently drops per-item on unknown types (P5/P7.1 scar tissue documented in workspace-schemas.ts); (e) docs/rules/recommendation-storage.md is a governed contract doc codifying the fallback — same-commit update required; (f) no WS-event work needed (read shape RecommendationSet is unchanged end-to-end), but state that explicitly as an acceptance line per audit §5/§88. Cross-ticket: R6 hard-depends on R7 completion (FK into recommendation_items is unenforceable while the fallback lives); R4's migration surface halves if R7 lands first (audit line 108); R0 (backup retention raise + restore drill) should precede the contract PR since migrations are forward-only with 3-day backup retention.
inventories.risks: Highest: prod data-state uncertainty — local counts (0 item rows) say nothing about prod, where post-158 regens have already written rows for some workspaces; mixed state is safe ONLY because of the count>0 guard, so the guard must survive review and be test-pinned. Second: silent per-item drops during backfill — parseJsonSafeArray drops malformed/enum-unknown recs with only a Pino warning; blob-vs-rows count divergence at the verify gate must be investigated, not tolerated (a missing RecType enum member would shed every rec of that type). Third: destructive contract step — blanking/dropping the blob is unrecoverable under forward-only migrations and 3-day backup retention; do not merge PR2 until the staging AND prod verify gate passes and R0's backup work is done. Do NOT attempt physical timestamp-column normalization across the 13 tables — 6 vocabularies × ~40 reader files is a blast radius far exceeding value; the registry abstraction gets the same invariant for free. Archive-twin SQL generation happens at module init: the PRAGMA read must run against a migrated DB — safe under the lazy createStmtCache pattern but must be tested against a fresh ephemeral-context DB where migrations just ran. Four snapshot tables have 0 local rows and the archive sweep has never fired anywhere observable — staging verification requires synthetic seeding or the PRs ship unexercised. ACCEPTANCE LINE (per audit §4.4, apply to every ticket): "WS payload + invalidation handlers + contract tests updated in the same PR" — the pinning tests are asymmetric: deliverable-ws-subscriber-wiring and mutation-lifecycle-invalidation assert EXACT source strings and EXACT key arrays (they break on cosmetic handler edits), while ws-invalidation-coverage/websocket-event-shapes only break on additions/renames.

MCP surface: 13 tool category files in server/mcp/tools/, 61 registered tools (62 `name:` grep hits incl. one non-tool), dispatched via server/mcp/server.ts, mounted at /mcp in server/app.ts (line 238, with dedicated rate-limit bucket); server/routes/mcp.ts does NOT exist (pr-check comment reference is stale). WHERE THE TOLERATE-OLD/EMIT-NEW LAYER LIVES: two halves. (a) Input tolerance — schema-level aliasing in shared/types/mcp-action-schemas.ts (z.preprocess/alias-map on the affected z.enum values), because that file is the single source for every tool inputSchema via toMcpJsonSchema (server/mcp/json-schema.ts). (b) Output emission + description text — a new server/mcp/vocab-compat.ts beside tool-helpers.ts, exporting mapOldToNew/emitBothAxes helpers consumed by the per-tool serializers (e.g. recToSummary). server/mcp/instructions.ts is the prose half of the same contract and is test-pinned (tests/unit/mcp-instructions.test.ts) — its own header mandates same-commit updates. server/mcp/README.md is the human contract doc. Neither docs/rules/strategy-recommendations.md nor docs/rules/platform-integration-surfaces.md mentions MCP at all (grep-verified 0 hits) — both are doc gaps the owning tickets (R4, R1/lexicon) should close.

KEY GAP FINDING (R4): MCP apply_recommendation 'send' calls sendRecommendation directly and does not implement the deliverable-spine branch that the P2 route handles for sendChannel==='deliverable' RecTypes (cannibalization) — the client_deliverable dual-write is apparently skipped on the MCP path. Needs an owner decision inside R4's scoping.

R2 and R5 as ratified are likely wire-neutral (TS rename / additive catalog) → their MCP lanes collapse to PR-1+PR-3 (verify-only). crosscuts.notes:
SCOPE: cross-cutting map of the intelligence-slice read layer (audit blind spot §4.5) per ticket; domain-lane files (recommendation storage, outcome store, migrations) are listed only where they are the single upstream of a slice read. All entries grep/read-verified this session against HEAD 3b1358af0 (branch readiness-atlas-audit); the audit ref e5745db2b is an ancestor with ZERO commits touching server/intelligence/, shared/types/intelligence.ts, server/outcome-tracking.ts, or server/domains/recommendations/ in between — the map is valid for both refs.

WHICH SLICES READ WHAT (the one-screen answer): (1) learnings-slice is the ONLY slice reading action_outcomes (via getActionsByWorkspace/getOutcomesForAction/getTopWinsFromActions; workspace-learnings.ts also reads both outcome tables for the summary) → R6/R9. (2) tracked_actions is also read by operational-slice (getPendingActions) and page-profile-slice (getActionsByPage) → R6. (3) The recommendation set (rows-with-blob-fallback via the loadRecommendations facade → domains/recommendations/storage.ts:183) is read by FOUR slices: operational (queue counts), seo-context (topRecommendation), page-profile (page recs), client-signals (recResponses/clientStatus) → R7/R4. No slice reads the blob column directly — the fork is entirely inside storage.ts. (4) client_signals is read only by client-signals-slice via server/client-signals-store.ts — and per the audit, R11's client_signals half (T6) was false at authorship, so NO R11 impact on that slice from T6. (5) Snapshot tables (13 confirmed in dev DB: audit, business_listing, competitor, ga4_conversion, llm_mention, local_visibility, performance, rank, redirect, roi, schema, serp, workspace_metrics — local-dev-not-prod caveat) are read by site-health (audit/redirect/performance/workspace_metrics), seo-context (competitor/llm_mention/rank), page-profile (audit/schema/rank/performance), local-seo (local_visibility/business_listing), client-signals (roi, +ga4 indirectly via roi.ts); serp_snapshots and ga4_conversion_snapshots have no direct slice read. (6) NO slice reads tracked_actions_archive/action_outcomes_archive (only outcome-tracking.ts + 2 tests).

AI-QUALITY FIXTURES TO RE-RUN (registry: scripts/ai-reliability-registry.ts, report via `npm run verify:ai-quality`, trace map docs/testing/ai-reliability-pipeline-trace-map.md): R4 → client-decision-support (scenario client-action-lifecycle-hygiene) + admin-insights-chat (learnings/clientSignals prompt blocks). R6 → client-decision-support, diagnostic-synthesis (diagnostic-synthesis-failure-contract emits outcomes), admin-insights-chat. R7 → content-brief-review (incl. seo-gen-quality-sparse-content-gaps + seo-gen-quality-malformed-ai-throws), admin-insights-chat, client-decision-support — rec-derived context blocks change population source. R9 → admin-insights-chat + content-brief-review + client-decision-support (formatLearnings prompt block gains the funnel line; deterministic-first rule: extend the registry, no live-model gate). R11 → full verify:ai-quality as regression only (no prompt-semantics change expected); diagnostic-synthesis if the audit-snapshot shape changes. If R9's funnel gets its own eval, extend scripts/ai-reliability-registry.ts — never a disconnected registry (docs/rules/ai-quality-evals.md).

PR-CHAIN SPLIT (staging-first, phase-per-PR, expand→backfill→staging-verify→contract for destructive changes; honors audit §6 R7-before-R4): R7: PR1 expand+backfill (batch materializeRecommendationItems across workspaces + invalidateIntelligenceCache per workspace + count parity report) → PR2 staging-verify (assemble all 4 rec-reading slices + one MCP get_workspace_intelligence + one AdminChat prompt on a rows-only workspace; add the slice-population contract assertion) → PR3 contract (delete legacyRecommendations fallback + blob strip). R6 (after R7): PR1 expand (source-snapshot columns on tracked_actions AND its archive twin + mappers + archive round-trip test) → PR2 snapshot-on-write in recordAction + backfill titles for resolvable sources + integrity-sweep job (background-job platform, report-only) → PR3 contract (typed source-ref union; outcomes route reads snapshot-first). R4 (after R7): PR1 DB CHECK/trigger + lifecycle sub-axis cleanup in shared/recommendation-predicates.ts + shared/types/recommendations.ts + strategy-recommendations.md same-commit → PR2 reader cleanup + slice/MCP verify. Keep R4 and R10 file-disjoint on recommendation-predicates/lifecycle. R9: PR1 coverage metric (aggregate helper + LearningsSlice field + formatter + tests + workspace-intelligence.md) with a separate staging flag-ON verification task for measured-capture (no code). R11: PR1 archive-twin alignment (small, independent) → PR2 envelope expand+backfill per table group (rename-to-archive + delayed drop; audit_snapshots workspace_id backfill needs a mapping source — flag as open question for the owner since the table currently has no workspace column) → PR3 staging-verify (all snapshot-consuming slices assemble + verify:ai-quality regression) → PR4 contract. EVERY data PR needs the audit's §4.5 acceptance line: 'slice read paths assembled against migrated data; WS invalidation fired; verify:ai-quality green'.

RISKS/FLAGS FOR THE PLANNER: (a) The R7 empty-read failure mode is silent by design — every slice part uses readOptionalSlicePart and degrades to empty/zeroed shapes, so a missed backfill produces no errors, just a quietly blind AI context; count-parity checks are mandatory, and the dev DB (0 recommendation_items rows, 13 workspaces) will make any local post-contract smoke look 'empty but healthy' — staging data required. (b) R6's new columns MUST be added to the hand-copied archive INSERT lists in outcome-tracking.ts in the same commit (positional-insert corruption precedent at lines 134–168) — this couples R6 to R11's T7 fix; do T7 alignment FIRST. (c) MCP is an external contract with persistent API keys — additive slice fields are safe, enum value changes are not. (d) tests/contract/intelligence-consumer-inventory.test.ts + intelligence-facade-cycles.test.ts + cycle-kill-boundaries.test.ts pin consumer inventory and dynamic-import discipline; new slice imports must stay lazy. (e) shared/types/intelligence.ts is a pre-commit shared contract under the multi-agent protocol — R9's LearningsSlice field must be committed before parallel dispatch.
